description: Lock Printed English Play Quartos to the 1594 artifact, print production, and theater lineage, not ticketing or optional rights context.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to the surviving first quarto of Titus Andronicus.
Theater remains the relevant performance and dramatic-text predecessor for printed scripts.
Theater is the dramatic-performance lineage for printed scripts, not a commercial scaling dependency.
Printed scripts still require print-publication infrastructure.
A printing privilege is optional legal context rather than a constitutive feature of the 1594 printed artifact.
Ticketing systems should not be restored unless the node is rescoped to theater admission commerce rather than printed play texts.
